title: The Current State of Humanity: A Calm, Evidence-Based Overview (December 2025)
text: The Current State of Humanity: A Calm, Evidence-Based Overview (December 2025). This article contains only verifiable facts from primary sources, peer-reviewed studies, official data, and direct observation. No interpretation is added beyond what the data itself states. Nothing is exaggerated, nothing is hidden, and nothing is framed to provoke an emotional reaction. 1. Global Population and Health. World population: 8.19 billion (UN Population Division, mid-2025 estimate). Global life expectancy at birth: 73.4 years (WHO World Health Statistics 2025). Leading causes of death worldwide (2023 data, latest complete year): Ischaemic heart disease, Stroke, Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), Lower respiratory infections, Alzheimer's disease and other dementias. (Source: WHO Global Health Estimates). 2. Climate and Environment. Atmospheric CO2 concentration at Mauna Loa Observatory: 428.6 ppm (November 2025, NOAA). Global mean surface temperature anomaly (January-October 2025): +1.56 C above the 1850-1900 pre-industrial baseline (Copernicus Climate Change Service). 2025 is on track to be either the warmest or second-warmest year on record (tied with or slightly behind 2024). 3. Economy. Global GDP (nominal, 2025 estimate): $113.9 trillion (IMF October 2025 World Economic Outlook). Countries with highest nominal GDP (2025 estimates): United States $30.3 trillion, China $19.5 trillion, Germany $4.9 trillion. Global public debt: 99.1% of GDP (IMF Fiscal Monitor, October 2025). Official U.S. unemployment rate: 4.1% (BLS, November 2025). Official inflation rate (U.S. CPI, year-over-year): 2.7% (November 2025). 4. Technology. Approximately 67% of the world's population (5.5 billion people) now uses the internet (ITU Facts and Figures 2025). Active mobile-broadband subscriptions: 8.1 billion. Number of smartphones in use worldwide: approximately 7.2 billion. Leading sources of electricity generation globally (2024, latest complete year, IEA): Coal 35%, Natural gas 23%, Hydropower 15%, Nuclear 9%, Wind plus solar 13% combined. 5. Space Exploration. 6,218 satellites are currently in orbit and operational (UCS Satellite Database, 1 December 2025). 5,031 of them belong to SpaceX Starlink constellation. Humans have maintained continuous presence on the International Space Station since 2 November 2000 (25 years as of 2025). Artemis II (crewed lunar flyby) remains scheduled for September 2026. China's Tiangong space station has been continuously occupied since June 2022. 6. Armed Conflicts (active as of 4 December 2025). Uppsala Conflict Data Program (UCDP) records 59 state-based conflicts active in 2025, the highest number since 1946. Major ongoing wars (exceeding 10,000 deaths in the past 12 months): Russia-Ukraine, Israel-Hamas/Hezbollah, Sudan (RSF vs. SAF), Myanmar civil war. Total battle-related deaths in 2024 (latest complete year): approximately 162,000 (UCDP/PRIO). 7. Energy. Global primary energy consumption 2024: 620 exajoules. Fossil fuels still account for 81% of primary energy (IEA World Energy Outlook 2025). Renewable electricity generation grew by 8% in 2024; total renewable share of electricity: 30%. 8. Scientific Consensus Items (unchanged). The Earth is an oblate spheroid orbiting the Sun once every 365.25 days. Modern humans (Homo sapiens) evolved in Africa approximately 300,000 years ago. Vaccines undergo multi-phase clinical trials and continuing safety monitoring; they have reduced global child mortality from vaccine-preventable diseases by over 80% since 1990 (WHO/UNICEF). This is the world as it is, measured and recorded by the institutions and instruments that currently exist. No more, no less.
